hero_desc: A local proxy tool purpose-built for complex network environments. TLS fragmentation, QUIC reconstruction, ECH injection — keep your traffic accessible even in heavily restricted networks.
adv_subtitle: Built native for Windows. No extra runtimes needed — works out of the box.
adv1_desc: Leverages Cloudflare's global CDN network directly as the exit point. No subscription fees, no server maintenance burden.
adv2_desc: TLS-RF, ECH, QUIC modes switching flexibly to match different censorship scenarios with excellent compatibility.
adv3_title: Native GUI — Truly Easy to Use
adv3_desc: Built with Wails + React as a native Windows application. Clean visuals, intuitive operation — no config file editing required.
tech_subtitle: Three-layer traffic obfuscation working in concert to build a highly resilient anti-censorship solution.
tech1_desc: TLS Fragmentation splits the Client Hello into multiple TLS record fragments. DPI devices typically only inspect the first fragment, hiding the real SNI.
tech2_desc: UDP-based QUIC naturally evades TCP fingerprint detection. We rebuild QUIC connections locally and encapsulate real traffic inside.
tech3_desc: Encrypted Client Hello encrypts what would otherwise be plaintext TLS extensions. Even the first step of the TLS handshake becomes invisible. Compatible with Cloudflare and other major ECH providers.
tech_mode1_desc: Deploy your own relay server on Cloudflare Workers or an overseas VPS — the ultimate solution for extreme censorship environments.
tech_mode2_desc: Combine multiple technologies, e.g. TLS-RF + Server relay, maximizing availability in heavily restricted networks.
feat_subtitle: Built for real-world usage in complex network environments.
feat1_desc: Built-in GFWList rule set — tens of thousands of blocked domains work out of the box.
feat2_desc: Automatically selects the lowest-latency node from Cloudflare's global IP pool.
feat3_desc: Configure DNS upstream servers with domain bypass support for intelligent DNS resolution.
feat4_desc: Virtual network adapter proxies all traffic system-wide — no per-app configuration needed.
feat5_desc: Supports domains, IP CIDR, and policy groups. Edit JSON and changes take effect instantly — no restart required.
feat6_desc: Built-in MITM proxy CA certificate installation wizard — one click to complete.
feat7_desc: Built-in node speed testing with intelligent optimal path selection.
feat8_desc: Smart routing and proxy rules for Discord, Twitch, XChat, and more.
faq1_q: Why do I need to install a root certificate?
faq1_a: SniShaper uses MITM (Man-in-the-Middle) proxy technology to intercept and analyze traffic for smart domain routing. The MITM proxy requires a custom CA root certificate to decrypt HTTPS traffic. Once installed, all certificates signed by SniShaper will be trusted by the system.
faq2_q: What's the difference between TLS-RF and ECH modes?
faq2_a1: (TLS Fragmentation) Splits the Client Hello into multiple TLS record fragments for transmission. Works for most scenarios without special server configuration.
faq2_a2: (Encrypted Client Hello) Fully encrypts the plaintext TLS extension parts for higher security, but requires the target server to support ECH (e.g. Cloudflare CDN).
faq3_q: How do I choose the right proxy mode?
faq3_l2: Highest protection level, ideal for Cloudflare sites
faq4_q: What's the difference between TUN mode and system proxy?
faq4_a1: Only affects applications that support proxy settings (e.g. browsers). Apps must actively use the proxy configuration.
faq4_a2: Captures all traffic via a virtual network adapter for system-wide proxy — no per-app configuration needed. Ideal for games and desktop apps that don't support proxy protocols.
faq5_q: How does Cloudflare IP picking work?
faq5_a: Cloudflare has millions of IP nodes worldwide. After fetching the IP pool via API, SniShaper automatically tests latency for each IP and selects the lowest-latency node. This significantly improves access speed and stability.
faq6_q: Is SniShaper open source and free?
faq6_a: Yes, SniShaper uses the MIT license and is completely free. Source code is hosted on GitHub and accepts community contributions. You're free to view, modify, and distribute the code.
qs_subtitle: Get started with SniShaper in three steps
qs_step1_desc: Download the latest version from this website, extract and double-click snishaper.exe to run.
qs_step2_desc: After first launch, go to the certificate management page, click "Install Root Certificate", and follow the system prompts.
qs_step3_desc: Select a proxy mode and click "Enable Proxy". Choose system proxy or TUN mode as needed.
qs_tip1: First-time users: TLS-RF mode offers the best compatibility
qs_tip2: System proxy works with browsers immediately, no extra config needed
qs_tip3: Games and desktop apps: enable TUN mode for system-wide proxy
qs_tip4: Enable auto-start and minimize to tray for the best experience
stack_desc: Wails v3 delivers a native Windows desktop experience. Go backend handles core logic — TLS obfuscation, QUIC rebuild, rule matching. React/TypeScript frontend provides a responsive GUI.
cl127_l1: Full SOCKS5 proxy protocol support with frontend/backend config UI and runtime management
cl127_l2: New Evolution Mode: AI-driven automatic rule generation, testing, and optimization with custom test cases
cl127_l3: New About page with core features, contributor list, and update checking
cl127_l5: Build script -Silent switch support, automatic admin elevation parameter passing
cl127_l6: Evolution Mode UI redesign: gradient header, progress bar animation, dark mode fixes
cl127_l7: sync.RWMutex replaces sync.Mutex for better concurrency performance
cl127_l8: File write permissions 0644→0600, RPC auth token mechanism, response body size limit, XSS protection
cl127_l9: Fixed Socks5 improper close causing memory leak
cl126_l1: DNS upstream server configuration with domain bypass support
cl126_l2: QUIC and ECH mode combination support
cl126_l6: Fixed numerous known issues and configuration inconsistencies
cl126_l7: Dependency upgrades, reduced polling frequency for performance optimization
cl126_l8: Smart rule editor: only shows relevant options based on current mode
cl126_l9: Discord, Twitch, and XChat support implemented
cl124_title: Dark Mode UI Fix & Config Separation
cl124_l1: Fixed dark mode UI display issues
cl124_l2: Rule config decoupled from user config, settings re-adjustment required
cl122_title: Experimental QUIC Direct & Full DoH Support
cl122_l1: Experimental QUIC direct connection support based on quic-go fragmentation
cl122_l2: Full DoH resolution with IPv4 / IPv6 selection
cl121_l1: External certificate verification logic added for domain fronting security
cl11x_l1: Fixed MITM mode HTTP page issues, added auto HTTPS redirect
cl11x_l2: Introduced TLS fragmentation mode for partial website support
cl11x_l3: Fixed Google configuration, now supports Google, YouTube, and Gemini
cl11x_l4: WARP as optional split routing, restoring access to sites like ChatGPT
cl11x_l5: ECH logic decoupled from Cloudflare for future multi-provider support
cl10_l1: Cloudflare ECH, parallel dialing, ECDSA certificates, and UI implemented
dl_desc: Download SniShaper for Windows. Open source, free, no registration required.
footer_desc: A local proxy tool for complex network environments. Using TLS fragmentation, QUIC reconstruction, ECH injection, and more to keep your traffic accessible in restricted networks.
footer_legal: SniShaper is designed to restore network access freedom. Please comply with local laws and use responsibly.
footer_notice: The original author has stepped away from development. The repository has been handed over to a community developer for continued maintenance.
